taxonomy: Alaticaulia acrochordonia is classified within the phylum Cnidaria, which includes species that have stinging cells called cnidocytes. The class is Cubozoa, a group of box jellyfish characterized by their distinctive box-shaped bell. Within the class Cubozoa, Alaticaulia acrochordonia is placed in the family Carybdeidae. This family is known for its highly toxic and venomous species. Further classification would involve the genus, but specific information on this aspect is limited.
